die: verb. To stop living; to become dead; to undergo death. followed by of as an indication of direct cause; general use:
dies: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of die
diesis: noun. Any of several intervals, smaller than a tone, in ancient Greek music.
dig: verb. To move hard-packed earth out of the way, especially downward to make a hole with a shovel.
disguise: noun. Material (such as clothing, makeup, a wig) used to alter one’s visual appearance in order to hide one's identity or assume another.
diss: verb. To put (someone) down, or show disrespect by the use of insulting language or dismissive behaviour.
disuse: noun. The state of not being used; neglect.
dug: verb. simple past and past participle of dig (replacing earlier digged)
ess: noun. The name of the Latin script letter S / s.
gid: noun. A disease caused by coenurosis of the brain, most commonly found in sheep and canids.
gie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guébie.
guess: verb. To reach a partly (or totally) unconfirmed conclusion; to engage in conjecture; to speculate.
guide: noun. Someone who guides, especially someone hired to show people around a place or an institution and offer information and explanation,
guids: noun. plural of guid; Alternative form of GUIDs.
guise: noun. A customary way of speaking or acting; a fashion, a manner, a practice (often used formerly in such phrases as "at his own guise";
guised: verb. simple past and past participle of guise
ides: noun. The notional full-moon day of a Roman month, occurring on the 15th day of the four original 31- day months (March, May, Quintilis or
issei: noun. A member of the first generation of Japanese immigrants to North America, South America, or Australia.
issue: noun. The action or an instance of flowing or coming out, an outflow, particularly: A movement of soldiers towards an enemy, a sortie.
issued: verb. simple past and past participle of issue.
side: noun. A bounding straight edge of a two-dimensional shape.
suds: noun. Lather; foam or froth formed by mixing soap and water.
sue: verb. To file a legal action against someone, generally a non-criminal (civil) action.
sued: verb. simple past and past participle of sue
sues: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of sue
used: verb. simple past and past participle of use
degu: noun. Any species of the genus Octodon, a small, caviomorph rodent native to Chile.
dei: interjection. Alternative spelling of dey (“informal term of address used when trying to get someone's attention.”).
gue: noun. A kind of fiddle or violin played on the Shetland Islands.
ide: noun. A freshwater fish of the family Cyprinidae, found across northern Europe and Asia, especially Leuciscus idus.
isis: noun. Any coral of the genus Isis, or family Isididae, composed of joints of white, stony coral, alternating with flexible, horny joints.
sed: verb. To edit a file or stream of text using sed.
sids: noun. Oat husks, or bran with some kernel, used to make sowens.
ssu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Susuami.
sud: noun. A bubble of lather or foam (the singular of suds).
sugi: noun. Wood of the Japanese cedar (Cryptomeria japonica).
suid: noun. Any of the family Suidae: a pig or a taxonomically related animal.
sus: noun. Suspicion (in terms of a sus law).
